name: canonify-app-authoring
description: A top-to-bottom, build-an-app-from-nothing tutorial for agents. Five chapters, one declaration verb each, taking an empty Canon org to a working app: schema.propose_migration → catalog.refine_object_type → state_machines.register → actions.declare → view_specs.declare. Complements the base `canonify` reference skill (which documents each verb in isolation) by walking the whole model-b authoring loop end to end with one worked CRM-generic example. Every command, REST path, and MCP tool here is real — verify with `canon actions list`.

resources.title: Chapter 1 — Bootstrap the schema
resources.description: Use schema.propose_migration to create your first tables. Auto-CRUD synthesises the default ObjectType + create/read/update/delete/list actions from the table shape, so one migration gives you a working data model on every surface.

resources.title: Chapter 2 — Refine the ObjectType
resources.description: Use catalog.refine_object_type to upgrade the permissive auto-default into a rich domain shape: narrow string columns to enums (the prerequisite for a StateMachine), add typed links between ObjectTypes with on_delete policies, surface hidden columns, and write human descriptions.

resources.title: Chapter 3 — Bind a StateMachine
resources.description: Use state_machines.register to bind a finite-state machine to an enum property. Transitions name the Action that drives them; on_enter/on_exit hooks enqueue follow-up actions. Registering the FSM is what lets your transition actions get a free guard + auto state-write.

resources.title: Chapter 4 — Declare a custom ActionType
resources.description: Use actions.declare to register a multi-step ActionType whose handler is a JSON DSL — typed CRUD steps over ObjectTypes glued by the $input / $<step> / $now / $principal ref grammar, executed inside the runtime's tx + audit + outbox + FSM frame.

resources.title: Chapter 5 — Compose a ViewSpec
resources.description: Use view_specs.declare to shape how the app renders: table, detail, panel, button, composite, metric, file, board, and queue. A ViewSpec shadows the renderer auto-default for an ObjectType and is validated against the live registry at declare time.

resources.title: Starter templates — reach the elevated treatment by default
resources.description: Copy-paste ViewSpec templates that already embody the elevated, well-crafted look — an elegant record detail (header + metric strip + one linked section), a scannable list (presets + emphasizeWhen + curated columns), a board, and a metric tile — plus a seven-move 'reach elegance' checklist. Every field is current vocabulary that validates today; adapt the ObjectType and property names to your app.
